data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration:

1. **TestProject.csproj**: 
   - Replace the three separate packages (`xunit.abstractions`, `xunit.extensibility.core`, `xunit.extensibility.execution`) with the single `xunit.v3.extensibility` package (or `xunit.v3` meta-package).
   - Replace `xunit.runner.visualstudio` with `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 or the new runner.
   - Actually, in xUnit v3, the runner model changed. Let me think about this more carefully.

   In xUnit v3:
   - `xunit.v3` is the main package that replaces the old `xunit` meta-package.
   - `xunit.v3.extensibility` consolidates the extensibility packages.
   - The abstractions are now part of `xunit.v3.common` or `xunit.v3.core` instead of a separate package.
   - The Visual Studio runner gets updated to `xunit.v3.runner.visualstudio`, or projects can become self-executable.
   - I'll also need to account for `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.

Actually, thinking through this more carefully: in v3, test projects can run directly as executables, which changes how the runner and SDK dependencies work. For Visual Studio Test Explorer integration specifically, you'd still need the v3 version of the Visual Studio runner, but the overall architecture is different from v2. For `dotnet test` support, xUnit v3 handles this differently since the test project is self-hosting as an executable, so the runner packages work differently than in v2. I need to verify the exact migration path for the runner integration, but the core package consolidation is clear: the separate abstractions and extensibility packages merge into `xunit.v3.extensibility`.

The namespace reorganization is significant—`Xunit.Abstractions` disappears entirely, with types like `ITestOutputHelper` moving directly into the `Xunit` namespace. For the project file, I'll need to set it as an executable rather than a library, which is a fundamental shift in how xUnit v3 projects are structured. I need to verify which xUnit v3 packages are actually available on NuGet, since I'm not entirely certain about the full package lineup.  For the global.json configuration, xUnit v3 likely uses a different test runner mechanism than the VSTest approach, so I may need to adjust or remove that runner setting depending on whether the project is self-hosting the test runner.

My plan is to update the project file to add the executable output type and swap out the v2 packages for the v3 extensibility package, then update the custom discoverer to remove the old Xunit.Abstractions namespace reference since those types have moved into the main Xunit namespace. After that I'll update global.json if necessary and build to verify everything works. I should check NuGet for the exact package names and versions before proceeding.

OK so the package is `xunit.v3` and the latest stable is `3.2.2`. But wait, that doesn't seem right. Let me reconsider.

Looking at the version list: `1.0.0`, `1.0.1`, `1.1.0`, `2.0.0`, ..., `3.0.0`, `3.0.1`, `3.1.0`, `3.2.0`, `3.2.1`, `3.2.2`, `4.0.0-pre.xxx`.

Hmm, but the package is called `xunit.v3`. So these versions are versions of the `xunit.v3` package itself, not xUnit.net versions. 

Actually, I need to clarify this. The `xunit.v3` package is the new package name for xUnit.net v3, while the older `xunit` package was for v2. The version numbers on `xunit.v3` started from earlier pre-release versions during development and have progressed up to `3.2.2` as the current stable release.

Now I'm trying to find the correct extensibility package name since `xunit.v3.extensibility` returned a 404 error. Let me check what the actual package names are for the core and common extensibility libraries.

Contents of https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/xunit.v3/3.2.2/xunit.v3.nuspec:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>xunit.v3</id>
    <version>3.2.2</version>
    <title>xUnit.net v3</title>
    <authors>jnewkirk,bradwilson</authors>
    <requireLicenseAcceptance>false</requireLicenseAcceptance>
    <license type="expression">Apache-2.0</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/Apache-2.0</licenseUrl>
    <icon>_content/logo-128-transparent.png</icon>
    <readme>_content/README.md</readme>
    <description>xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://xunit.net/releases/v3/3.2.2</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>Copyright (C) .NET Foundation</copyright>
    <repository type="git" url="https://github.com/xunit/xunit" commit="728c1dce012cd82193035dddfeaba184baaa88c6" />
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETFramework4.7.2">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.mtp-v1" version="[3.2.2]" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.mtp-v1" version="[3.2.2]" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
  </metadata>
</package>

<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>xunit.v3.extensibility.core</id>
    <version>3.2.2</version>
    <title>xUnit.net v3 [Core Unit Testing Framework Extensibility]</title>
    <authors>jnewkirk,bradwilson</authors>
    <requireLicenseAcceptance>false</requireLicenseAcceptance>
    <license type="expression">Apache-2.0</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/Apache-2.0</licenseUrl>
    <icon>_content/logo-128-transparent.png</icon>
    <readme>_content/README.md</readme>
    <description>Includes the core library (xunit.v3.core.dll), to be used for extensibility; test authors should include xunit.v3.core instead. Supports .NET Standard 2.0.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://xunit.net/releases/v3/3.2.2</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>Copyright (C) .NET Foundation</copyright>
    <repository type="git" url="https://github.com/xunit/xunit" commit="728c1dce012cd82193035dddfeaba184baaa88c6" />
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard2.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.common" version="[3.2.2]" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
  </metadata>
</package>

data.content: Now I have a clear picture. Let me implement the migration.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.abstractions" Version="2.0.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.core" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.execution" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Here's the migration plan:

**Package changes:**
- `xunit.abstractions` 2.0.3 → removed (absorbed into v3 packages)
- `xunit.extensibility.core` 2.9.3 → `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` 3.2.2 (for extensibility authors)
- `xunit.extensibility.execution` 2.9.3 → removed (no separate execution package in v3; core covers it)
- `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 2.8.2 → removed (v3 is self-hosting via MTP)


- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` 18.3.0 → removed (v3 uses MTP, not VSTest)

Wait, I need to reconsider this. Looking at the actual code in `CustomDiscoverer.cs`, it's importing from `Xunit` and `Xunit.Abstractions`, using `ITestOutputHelper`. This is a test project that also implements custom discovery logic, so it's both a test runner and an extensibility consumer. That means I need to keep the test SDK and runner packages for the test execution side, while also handling the extensibility migration.

For xUnit v3, the question is whether to use `xunit.v3` as the main package (which brings in the MTP runner and core) or if the extensibility APIs require a different approach. The `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` package exists separately from `xunit.v3.core`, so I need to figure out which one this project actually needs and whether they can coexist. to `xunit.extensibility.core`, it seems like this is meant to be a test support library rather than a test project itself. The `OutputLogger` class is just a helper wrapping `ITestOutputHelper`, so I don't need to make it an executable. I should stick with `xunit.v3` as the main package reference since it provides everything needed for a test project, and the extensibility types will come through transitively. me think through the actual migration steps. In xUnit v3, test projects become executables with source generators handling the entry point, so I need to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and consolidate all those packages into just `xunit.v3` 3.2.2. Then I'll update the namespace references in `CustomDiscoverer.cs` since `ITestOutputHelper` moves to the `Xunit` namespace, and check what adjustments the `global.json` needs for the test runner configuration.

    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.abstractions" Version="2.0.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.core" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.execution" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.abstractions" Version="2.0.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.core" Version="2.9.3" />

    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>", "new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

    <PackageReference Include="xunit.v3" Version="3.2.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>"}

data.content: Build succeeded. Let me verify the final state of all files:

Migration complete. Here's what changed:

**`TestProject.csproj`**
- Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` — xUnit v3 projects are self-hosting executables
- Replaced all 5 package references (`xunit.abstractions`, `xunit.extensibility.core`, `xunit.extensibility.execution`, `xunit.runner.visualstudio`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) with a single `xunit.v3` 3.2.2

**`CustomDiscoverer.cs`**
- Removed `using Xunit.Abstractions;` — `ITestOutputHelper` now lives in the `Xunit` namespace

**`global.json`**
- Changed test runner from `VSTest` to `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` (MTP), which xUnit v3 uses natively
